title: Java HashSet
content:
### HashSet in Java

HashSet is a collection that stores unique elements. It is backed by a HashMap internally.

---

### Creating a HashSet

```java
import java.util.HashSet;

HashSet<String> names = new HashSet<>();
HashSet<Integer> numbers = new HashSet<>(100);  // initial capacity
HashSet<String> copy = new HashSet<>(otherSet);
```

---

### Common Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `add(element)` | Add element (false if exists) |
| `remove(element)` | Remove element |
| `contains(element)` | Check if element exists |
| `size()` | Number of elements |
| `isEmpty()` | Check if empty |
| `clear()` | Remove all elements |

---

### Iterating

```java
for (String s : set) {
    System.out.println(s);
}
```

---

### HashSet Characteristics

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| No duplicates | Adding duplicate returns false |
| Unordered | No guaranteed iteration order |
| Null allowed | One null element |
| Not synchronized | Use Collections.synchronizedSet |
| Fast operations | O(1) average for add, remove, contains |

---

### Set Operations

```java
HashSet<Integer> set1 = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(1, 2, 3));
HashSet<Integer> set2 = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList(2, 3, 4));

set1.addAll(set2);      // Union
set1.retainAll(set2);   // Intersection
set1.removeAll(set2);   // Difference
```

> **Key Point:** HashSet relies on `hashCode()` and `equals()` to detect duplicates. Elements must properly override these methods for correct behavior.

quiz.question: What happens when you add a duplicate element to HashSet?
What data structure backs a HashSet?
